extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won Stage 4 after BERNAL Egan initiated late attacks on the final climb but refused to commit, allowing Valverde to outsprint him.
tactical_patterns: LATOUR Pierre embedded within Movistar group early to surveil VALVERDE rather than sitting with own team
BERNAL Egan repeatedly accelerated on final climb but looked back repeatedly, lacking conviction to fully commit solo
VALVERDE sat on BERNAL's wheel through multiple accelerations, conserving energy for inevitable sprint finish
MOHORIČ Matej attacked solo before final climb when big teams hesitated to chase, testing peloton's coordination

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: won despite being forced to respond to multiple BERNAL attacks, showing superior positioning discipline over raw power
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: attacked elite field repeatedly on terrain not suited to him, suggests Tour preparation prioritizing aggression over results
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: infiltrated rival team group for surveillance rather than pacing own leaders, tactical maturity rare for young rider
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: paced through final climb but never challenged for stage, possibly managing efforts with Movistar having multiple cards
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: struggled to hold contact with lead group on climb he won previous year, form regression evident
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: finished in chase group without attempting to bridge, suggests targeting later mountain stages
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: launched solo preemptive attack before climb despite being heavy rouleur, tactical intelligence over physiological fit
